exemplar.title: To what extent does authoritarian parenting style influence decision-making in young adolescents?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent does authoritarian parenting style influence decision-making in young adolescents?

subject.title: Psychology
subject.description:
IB Psychology is a Group 3 subject in the International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP). The course examines human behavior and mental processes through scientific inquiry, exploring biological, cognitive, and sociocultural approaches to understanding behavior. Students develop critical thinking skills while investigating contemporary psychological research, research methods, and ethical considerations in psychological practice.

The course is available at Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours) and Higher Level (HL, 240 hours), with first assessment in 2027.

Assessment consists of Paper 1 (integration of concepts, content, and contexts through short-answer and extended-response questions: SL 2 hours, HL 2 hours, SL 50%, HL 40%), Paper 2 (application of psychological knowledge to research contexts: SL 1 hour, HL 2 hours, SL 25%, HL 20%), Paper 3 (HL only: research methods and data analysis, 1 hour, 20%), and Internal Assessment (research proposal development: SL 25%, HL 20%).

strengths: Explicit, focused research question consistently anchors the essay
Clear introduction that isolates the central topic and establishes significance
Literature sections regularly linked back to the research question, maintaining coherence
weaknesses: Methodological rationale is under-articulated (no clear search strategy or inclusion criteria)
Occasional drift into comparisons with other parenting styles dilutes tight focus
Conclusion introduces new variables without prior integration
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place two elements solidly in the 5â6 band and one in the 3â4 band. The clear communication and focused research question merit a high mark, but the incomplete articulation of the methodology prevents a full 6. A score of 5 reflects the strong focus and method overall while acknowledging the methodological transparency issues.

strengths: Sound summary and application of core theories (Baumrind, Bandura, dual-process models)
Detailed presentation of methodological designs in key studies
Integration of empirical results with theoretical constructs shows coherent understanding
weaknesses: Occasional misuse or conflation of terminology (e.g. authoritative vs authoritarian)
Spelling and grammar lapses (âdelinguencyâ, âquidanceâ) reduce precision
Inconsistencies in term usage (e.g. âNeglectiveâ vs âNeglectfulâ)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Knowledge and Understanding strands earned upper-band performance in content and mid-band in terminology. The student demonstrates good understanding and mostly accurate application of sources, but inconsistencies in terminology and minor conceptual inaccuracies limit the score to 4 out of 6.

strengths: Use of seven diverse, peer-reviewed studies shows extensive and relevant research
Critical acknowledgement of self-report bias and other methodological limitations
Cross-study synthesis (e.g. Alexander vs Sovet & Metz) demonstrates analytical depth
Consideration of cultural moderation adds nuanced evaluation
weaknesses: Research approach appears opportunistic rather than systematic
Analysis often remains surface-level, with unsupported or under-developed claims
Conflicting findings are listed rather than fully reconciled into a coherent argument
Limited critical evaluation of instrumentsâ psychometric properties
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The studentâs critical-thinking performance is solidly mid-to-high range: research is relevant and broad, and analysis is generally clear, but deeper synthesis and systematic evaluation are lacking. An 8/12 balances evident strengths against the need for more rigorous, integrated critique.

strengths: Logical overall structure with introduction, body and conclusion
Readable layout with headings and a standard reference list
weaknesses: Missing key elements (abstract, table of contents) and no stated word count
Visible editing codes and inconsistent in-text citation disrupt flow
Reference entries and pagination lack uniform formatting
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation meets basic conventions but omits several required EE elements and shows formatting inconsistencies. The work sits at the top of the 1â2 band, warranting a 2 out of 4 to reflect a generally coherent structure with notable layout flaws.

strandSelections.reasoning:
Subject-specific vocabulary (e.g., "core self-evaluation," "career decision self-efficacy," "collectivistic ideals") is employed accurately in many sections, showing familiarity with psychological terminology.  
â¢ However, spelling/grammar lapses (âdelinguency,â âquidanceâ) and occasional concept conflation (authoritative vs authoritarian) reduce consistency.  
â¢ The work therefore fulfils âmostly accurateâ use rather than fully consistent mastery, placing it in the 3-4 band; a mid-band score of 3 is appropriate.

Formatting shows attention to headings and a reference list, but the EE lacks a stated word count, employs inconsistent in-text citation, and contains visible editing codes (overlines, page markers).  
â¢ Page numbers are not apparent, yet overall presentation is readable and the reference section follows a standard convention.  Accordingly, âsome layout considerations missing or incorrectâ applies, positioning the work at 2 within the 1-2 band.

The essay summarises core psychological theories (Baumrindâs taxonomy, Banduraâs self-efficacy, dual-process decision models) and applies them to the selected studies.  
â¢ Descriptions of sampling, scales (PAQ, CDSE-SF) and cultural moderation demonstrate sound content knowledge.  
â¢ Some inaccuracies appearâfor example, confusing âauthoritativeâ with âauthoritative/authoritarianâ in the Tian study and occasional misuse of terms such as âsystems 1/2â outside their cognitive contextâbut overall understanding is clear and source material is mostly well integrated.  This merits the upper end of the 3-4 band, scored at 4.

The student adopts a secondary-research methodology, collating peer-reviewed quantitative studies from differing cultures and outlining sample sizes, instruments, and procedures.  
â¢ Source selection is generally relevant, but the rationale for inclusion (why these seven studies were chosen over others, how databases were searched) is not articulated, and no clear analytical framework is described.  
â¢ Referencing is uneven and there is no direct discussion of ethical or methodological criteria beyond brief critiques, so the work meets descriptors for a âmostly completeâ rather than âcompleteâ methodology; a low 3 within the 3-4 band is suitable.

The essay opens with a clearly-stated title and an introductory section that explains the four parenting styles, isolates the authoritarian style as the central focus, and establishes the link to adolescent decision-making.  
â¢ Key psychological constructs (parental control, self-efficacy, System 1/System 2 thinking) are introduced early so the reader understands why the topic is significant.  
â¢ Minor language issues and occasional digressions reduce overall precision, yet the purpose and direction remain unambiguous throughout.  Consequently the work satisfies the descriptor of âcommunicated accurately and effectively,â meriting placement in the 5-6 band; a mid-band mark of 5 is appropriate given the small lapses noted.

Seven empirical studies from diverse cultural contexts (China, France, Korea, Turkey, US, Indonesia) are reviewed, all directly addressing the relationship between parenting style and adolescent career decision variables.  
â¢ The sources are recent and peer-reviewed, demonstrating relevance; cultural breadth enhances strength.  
â¢ Nonetheless, the search appears opportunistic rather than systematic, and at times the essay relies heavily on secondary commentary.  This aligns with âgood research mostly relevant,â warranting a mid-band 5 within the 4-6 range.

The research questionââTo what extent does authoritarian parenting style influence decision-making in young adolescents?ââis explicit, focused, and consistently referenced.  
â¢ Each literature section links back to this question, illustrating how authoritarian control affects career self-efficacy or decisional difficulty.  
â¢ Although some discussion drifts into comparisons with authoritative parenting, the core focus is maintained, justifying the upper band.  A score of 5 (rather than 6) recognises occasional widening of scope that slightly dilutes the tightness of the focus.

The essay follows a recognisable EE structure (introduction, body organised by study, conclusion, references), but lacks an abstract, contents page and clear sub-headings for each criterion, so some required elements are missing.  
â¢ Pagination artefacts (numeric markers {0}, {1}â¦) interrupt flow, yet main sections remain identifiable.  Hence structure is âgenerally appropriate,â earning the top of the 1-2 band (score 2).

The discussion culminates in a balanced conclusion that culture moderates authoritarian parentingâs impact; alternative variables (school, socio-economic status) are acknowledged.  
â¢ The argument is logical but not fully synthesised; contradictory findings are juxtaposed rather than systematically reconciled, and no clear judgment on âextentâ is quantified.  
â¢ Evaluation of evidence quality is present but partial, fitting the descriptor of âreasoned argument with partially critical evaluation.â A score of 4 within the 4-6 band is justified.

The student summarises findings, compares cultural differences, and notes methodological limitations (self-report bias, limited generalisability).  
â¢ Some evaluative comments are insightfulâe.g., recognising Western career theoriesâ individualistic biasâbut many critiques remain surface-level and unsupported by deeper methodological analysis.  
â¢ Logical progression exists but occasionally slips into description.  This equates to âclear analysis with mostly supported conclusions,â meriting a low-mid 4 in the 4-6 band.

annotations.comment: Title is clearly stated, meeting formatting requirements and ensuring immediate clarity of the investigationâs scope.
The research question is explicit and focused, effectively anchoring the inquiry throughout the essay.
Spelling error âdelinguencyâ undermines credibility; proofread subject-specific terminology for greater precision.
Refine the methodological focus statement to specify the scope and limitations of secondary research early on.
Classification in Tian study of âpositive (authoritative)â vs ânegative (authoritative, subjective, and negative)â is confusing; correct labeling.
Expand analysis of findings in this section with reference to theoretical frameworks to deepen critical insight.
Acknowledging self-report bias shows critical awareness of methodological limitations, strengthening the evaluation.
Synthesize conflicting study results rather than listing them to strengthen argumentative coherence and flow.
Detailed methodology of Sovet & Metzâs cross-cultural design demonstrates thorough engagement with research conventions.
Lacks rationale for selecting specific scales; justify instrument choices to support validity and relevance.
Indulgent style section truncates mid-sentence, disrupting coherence. Ensure all paragraphs are complete and flow logically.
Uses âNeglectiveâ instead of standard âNeglectfulâ; refine terminology to align with psychological literature.
The binary âpositiveâ vs. ânegativeâ classification conflates parenting styles, causing conceptual confusion; clarify categories.
Accurate explanation of self-efficacy theory demonstrates strong understanding of key psychological constructs.
Clarify search strategy and inclusion criteria for selected studies to strengthen methodological transparency.
Add an abstract and table of contents preceding the main body to adhere to extended essay conventions.
Elaborate on how culture moderates authoritarian outcomes to add depth to the studyâs conclusion.
Inclusion of diverse sample demographics in Cenkseven et al. is well-presented, adding credibility to the evidence base.
Critically evaluate instrumentsâ psychometric properties to bolster discussion of reliability and validity.
This summary drifts from the central research question, reducing focus; align discussion more directly with the RQ.
Comparative analysis linking Alexander et al. to Sovet & Metz adds valuable cross-study synthesis.
Contradictory findings are presented without reconciliation; integrate evidence to avoid fragmented argumentation.
Clear articulation of Keller studyâs sampling enhances credibility of reported results.
Explore potential influence of religious context on self-efficacy outcomes to strengthen interpretation.
Integration of parental behavior theories with empirical results displays strong conceptual linkage.
Acknowledgement of potential parent-report bias reflects nuanced critical evaluation of methodology.
Statement about control and decision-making lacks supporting evidence; provide data or citations to substantiate.
Salim & Preston methodological overview is succinct and informative, demonstrating clear source comprehension.
Paragraph breaks mid-sentence, indicating incomplete thought; revise to ensure full and coherent exposition.
Conclusion introduces new variables without integration into earlier analysis; maintain coherence by linking back to the main discussion.
Reference formatting is inconsistent (period placement, italics); standardize to a recognized citation style.
Ensure each reference includes access dates and complete publisher details for completeness and reliability.
Include page numbers in the header or footer to meet formatting guidelines and aid navigation.
Reference entries wrap irregularly and lack uniform indentation; improve layout consistency in the bibliography.
